confirmed.symptom: 7 boolean fields changed from 'gorm:"default:true"' to 'gorm:"not null"' without providing default values. This causes GORM migrations to not populate these columns with default values, leaving them NULL for new rows created after the migration.
confirmed.root_cause: Incomplete GORM schema update: removed gorm:default:true tags without replacing with gorm:"default:true;not null" or ensuring database-level defaults. Affected fields: CanStream, CanCreatePlaylists, ResumePlayback, ShowAnalytics, ShowContinueWatching, ShowRecommended, ShowTrending, ShowBufferBar, DownloadPrompt.

confirmed.symptom: Incorrect pointer initialization: progress.CompletedAt = new(time.Now()). The expression new(time.Now()) calls new() on the return value of time.Now() (a time.Time value), not on a type. This produces undefined behavior and may panic or produce incorrect values.
confirmed.root_cause: Misuse of Go's new() builtin for value initialization. Correct pattern is: t := time.Now(); progress.CompletedAt = &t

suspect.symptom: Query parameter 'search' is truncated to 200 runes via truncateQuery() helper. Frontend may send queries longer than 200 chars, but they will be silently truncated without user notification.
suspect.note: This is intentional (security fix for LIKE fan-out), but frontend users should be aware of the 200-char limit. Verify if there's client-side validation matching this limit.

suspect.symptom: type_counts is only populated when params.type === 'all', but the v-show/filter logic on lines 918 and 977 uses typeCounts without null-coalescing. If API response lacks type_counts field or user switches away from type=all, rendering may incorrectly hide tabs.
suspect.note: Verify: Is typeCounts.value re-initialized when type filter changes away from 'all'? Is type_counts always present in response?

suspect.symptom: scheduleUnregisterUpload launches a goroutine that reads m.done without holding a lock. While this is by design (done is chan struct{}, which is safe to read concurrently), verify that the channel is always closed on module shutdown to avoid goroutine leaks.
suspect.note: Review: Module.Stop() calls m.doneOnce.Do(func() { close(m.done) }). This is correct pattern. Status: SAFE but warrants verification.

false_positive.reason: Upload progress CompletedAt field usage: while new(time.Now()) is syntactically incorrect, it may not actually cause a crash in some Go versions due to escape analysis. However, it is still dangerous and should be fixed to t := time.Now(); &t for clarity and correctness.
ContentTab.vue timer leak: The fix correctly stores scanRefreshTimer and clears it in onUnmounted(). Analysis confirmed the fix is complete and working as intended. No actual leak present.

Upload quota race in UploadMedia handler: The code correctly re-reads fresh storage from auth module (lines 103-106, 220-223) before quota checks, then uses AddStorageUsed atomically. Pattern is sound despite concurrent upload window.

recent_fixes_verified: Admin timer leak (ContentTab.vue scanRefreshTimer): VERIFIED FIXED
Search query cap at 200 chars (handlers): VERIFIED IMPLEMENTED
Upload progress bar real-time tracking: VERIFIED (proper per-upload mutex pattern)

summary: Static analysis identified 2 confirmed critical/high-severity bugs: (1) pointer initialization bug in upload.go line 294 (new(time.Now())); (2) GORM schema migration bug affecting 9 boolean fields in models.go that lost default values. Additionally, 1 suspect field-access pattern in index.vue requires verification. Recent security fixes (timer leak, search cap) are confirmed implemented correctly.
